summary:
A) The Chamfer finish line is primarily used for creating a marginal ridge on the lingual surface during crown preparation because of better post-placement visualization and easier application there versus the facial aspect. Given its purpose, this technique enhances fit and aesthetics but may not always provide superior results or indications when placed on other areas such as incisal edges unless specified by specific treatment needs. To directly answer your query about common usage:

The Chamfer finish line is most frequently utilized on the lingual surface of teeth undergoing crown preparations given improved visual assessment capabilities upon completion relative to anteriorly positioned faces which might obscure proper evaluation once restored with porcelain facings among others. 

This summary focuses only on critical aspects related to answering why one area over another receives these finishes based purely off provided information without extraneous details while maintaining
